hero_subtitle: Khao Pad is a multilingual CMS template that runs on Cloudflare Workers, D1, R2, and KV. One SvelteKit app — a fast public site at `/`, a full admin panel at `/cms`. Fork it, paste in your Cloudflare IDs, deploy. Lighter than Strapi, cheaper than Supabase, stays on Cloudflare.
hero_meta_i18n: EN + TH out of the box
features_title: Everything a small editorial team needs.
features_subtitle: No drag-and-drop builder. No page hierarchy. Just a clean content model, a markdown editor that doesn't fight you, and a public site that's fast.
f_editor_title: A markdown editor that respects your hands
f_editor_body: Toolbar, split-pane preview, ⌘B / ⌘I / ⌘K shortcuts, image picker tied to your R2 library, autosave to localStorage with a recovery banner if your laptop dies mid-paragraph.
f_i18n_body: Articles, categories, and tags each have per-locale title and body. The slug stays English-only and shared, so URLs don't fork. Paraglide compiles UI strings to type-safe functions — no runtime dictionary lookups.
f_storage_body: Articles live in D1, media in R2 — both behind a ContentProvider interface. The default D1 provider is shipped and battle-tested. A GitHub-backed provider for git-versioned content is on the roadmap, sitting behind the same interface so the swap is one env var, not a rewrite.
f_auth_body: Email + password, D1-backed sessions, four roles out of the box (super_admin → author). First sign-up auto-promotes to super_admin and locks the door behind it.
f_deploy_title: Real staging + production from day one
f_deploy_body: Push main → staging deploy. Tag v1.2.3 → production deploy. Each environment has its own D1/R2/KV bindings. The workflow runs migrations before the worker goes live.
f_template_title: It's a template, not a framework
f_template_body: Click "Use this template", or fork the example repo for a working demo with sample content. Stay in sync with upstream via a weekly sync PR — review and merge on your schedule.
demo_title: A live demo with five fried-rice articles, two locales, one click.
demo_subtitle: The demo is a real fork of the template, deployed to Cloudflare Workers with the same pipeline you'll use. Browse the public site, sign in to the CMS, edit an article, hit save — refresh the public route and your edit is live.
demo_credentials_body: Visit /register on the demo to claim the first super-admin account. After that, sign-up locks itself.
stack_subtitle: Khao Pad doesn't ship a runtime, an ORM, or an auth library. It composes the ones you already trust.
cta_subtitle: Click the demo, sign in, edit a fried-rice recipe. If you like what you feel, fork the template and paste in your Cloudflare IDs. We documented every step.
footer_tagline: An open-source CMS for Cloudflare. Built in Bangkok.
footer_made_with: Made with Khao Pad. MIT licensed.
